terms.definition: A medieval Ethiopian ruling line from the Agaw people of Lasta (c. 900–1270 AD), renowned for constructing the rock-hewn churches of Lalibela.
The 'Glory of Kings', a 14th-century national epic asserting the Solomonic lineage of Ethiopian kings, from Menelik I (son of Solomon and Sheba) bringing the Ark of the Covenant to Ethiopia.
The metropolitan bishop and spiritual head of the Ethiopian Orthodox Tewahedo Church, traditionally a Coptic monk appointed by the Patriarch of Alexandria.
A monastic renewal movement founded by Abba Ewostatewos that emphasized strict discipline and sparked a theological debate by insisting on observing both the Saturday Sabbath and Sunday.
A legendary Christian patriarch and king believed by medieval Europeans to rule a lost, powerful Christian nation in the East or Africa, eventually associated with Ethiopia.
A major Ethiopian Christian festival celebrating the 'Finding of the True Cross' by Empress Helena, deeply promoted by Emperor Zara Yaqob.
A powerful Muslim state in the Horn of Africa (c. 1415–1577) that was the primary geopolitical and religious rival to the Christian Ethiopian Empire.
